senses.gloss: The twenty-third letter of the Arabic alphabet, written in the Arabic script; preceded by ك (k) and followed by م (m).
The twelfth numeral symbol of the Arabic alphabet (traditional abjad order, used for list numbering), written in the Arabic script; preceded by ك (k) and followed by م (m).

examples.english: Oh for mother to the children! (Oh mother, help your children!)

senses.gloss: to, in order to: followed by the subjunctive mood

examples.english: I entered the kitchen to drink water.

senses.gloss: used with the verbs أَرَادَ (ʔarāda) and أَمَرَ (ʔamara)

examples.text: إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ اللّٰهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ
examples.english: Allah only intends to purify you of impurity.

examples.text: فَالْتَقَطَهُ آلُ فِرْعَوْنَ لِيَكُونَ لَهُمْ عَدُوًّا وَحَزَنًا
examples.english: And so the family of the Pharaoh picked him up, only to become an enemy and a grief.

senses.gloss: Used to introduce an imperative with a jussive verb; let

examples.text: لِتَأْخُذُوا مَنَاسِكَكُمْ فَإِنِّي لَا أَدْرِي لَعَلِّي لَا أَحُجُّ بَعْدَ حَجَّتِي هَذِهِ
examples.english: Learn your rituals from me, for I do not know—perhaps this will be my last Hajj.

examples.text: وَقُلِ ٱلْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ فَمَنْ شَاءَ فَلْيُؤْمِنْ وَمَنْ شَاءَ فَلْيَكْفُرْ
examples.english: And say, "The truth is from your Lord, so whoever wills, let him believe; and whoever wills, let him disbelieve."
And indeed, the weakest of homes is the home of the spider.
Verily, thy Lord is on the lookout.

etymology.text:
From the Nabataean Aramaic letter 𐢑 (l, “lāmaḏ”), derived from the Phoenician letter 𐤋 (l, “lāməd”), from the Egyptian hieroglyph 𓌅. See also Classical Syriac ܠ (l, “lāmaḏ”), Hebrew ל (l, “lamed”), Ancient Greek Λ (L), Greek Λ (L), Latin L.

From Proto-Semitic *lV-.
